must_detect.matched_text:
Term Sheet — Proposed Strategic Investment in Ficus Marine Holdings Ltd. (SGX Mainboard; ticker: FMHL) by Orchard Venture Partners Pte. Ltd.
Public disclosure and MNPI: This summary aligns with the issuer’s previously published SGXNET announcements and does not contain MNPI under the SFA or SGX Listing Rules.
Structure: Primary issuance of new ordinary shares and a secondary purchase from existing non‑controlling shareholders, for an aggregate stake not exceeding approximately 12%, subject to shareholder approval; no price terms are set out in this summary.
Conditions precedent: SGX approval (if required), shareholders’ approval at EGM, MAS/SFA compliance confirmations, completion of confirmatory due diligence limited to non‑price‑sensitive matters.
Data protection schedule: Schedule DPA‑1 (Processor Terms) and Annex Privacy‑Ops form part of this term sheet; controls follow PDPA (Cap. 26) obligations on purpose limitation, notification, and accountability.
Minimisation and retention: Personal data fields in diligence rooms are limited to role‑based contact points; retention for diligence artifacts is capped at 6 years per Policy PD‑RET‑06, after which erasure procedures apply; these are policy statements and not notices of any breach.
Cross‑border transfer approval (intentional positive): A one‑time export of an employee contact directory to a regional HRIS vendor in Malaysia has been approved under internal Data Export Approval DE‑2025‑0145, with transfer restrictions and contractual clauses mapped in DPA Schedule 2.
DSAR and consent withdrawal: There are no outstanding DSARs; the last two requests (ex‑employee access and marketing consent withdrawal) were completed and closed in accordance with Policy PD‑IND‑03; this line records closure, not an open incident.
Erasure status: Routine quarterly erasure of obsolete applicant CVs in the diligence staging folder was completed and logged (Batch ER‑Q1‑2025); no live erasure failures are reported.
Onboarding forms and notices: Employee onboarding Form OF‑EMP‑2025 includes PDPA collection notices and a plain‑language consent statement; unnecessary identity‑number fields are removed as part of minimisation.
Confidentiality and clean team: Only aggregated or role‑based data is accessible to Orchard’s diligence team; no customer PII is shared and any MNPI review occurs after public disclosure or within a ring‑fenced clean team under Annex CT‑1.
Employment and integration: No automatic transfer of employment; any post‑completion hires will use the standard PDPA‑compliant onboarding process with privacy notices and optional marketing consent boxes unchecked by default.
Representations: The issuer represents that, as at signing, it is not aware of any unresolved PDPA enforcement notices and has implemented a data breach response plan; references to “erasure,” “DSAR,” and “consent withdrawal” in this term sheet reflect policy controls, not active incidents.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
